title: Decentralized per-project state with a shared engine, not a monolithic hub
context: Deciding how to deploy the framework across many projects.

options.name: monolithic-hub
options.rationale: One place holds all queues and decisions; central observability.
options.tradeoff: Centralizes memory away from the code it documents; concentrates secrets and blast radius; forces broad cross-repo access.

options.name: decentralized-plugin
options.rationale: Shared engine installed once; each project owns its state and decision log.
options.tradeoff: A small per-project scaffold to init.

reasoning: The decision log must travel with the code it documents; per-project isolation and scoped repo access suit a regulated L&W context and shrink blast radius. The worker fleet can still be centralized while state stays per-project.
